rows.notes: Page title at top left is crisply written and fully legible: Roman II, '(Capital)' and '1900'. No monetary columns present on this row.

Monetary columns are well aligned with the vertical ruling lines; pounds/shillings/pence read as '53 17 6' and all values fall in valid ranges. Description handwriting is moderately unclear (the middle word is abbreviated/stylised); interpreted as 'Div. Comp. Fund' based on letter shapes and common ledger phrasing.

Description is clearly legible as 'Balance at close of acct'. Amounts read cleanly in aligned columns as '8696 13 2'. Values validate (shillings 13 within 0-19, pence 2 within 0-11). Slight ink variation on the thousands digit reduces perfect certainty.

Total boxed and clearly written as '8750 10 8' with a preceding pound symbol; aligns with the sum of the two above rows (53 + 8696 plus carry from 30 shillings = 8750 pounds, 10s, 8d). Vertical ruling lines used for precise column placement; numerals crisp and unambiguous.
